meta.name: prompt
meta.title: Prompt Skill
meta.description: Guidelines for creating high-quality prompt files for GitHub Copilot Triggers on: **/*.prompt.md

original_definition.skill_description: Guidelines for creating high-quality prompt files for GitHub Copilot Triggers on: **/*.prompt.md

actions.name: Provide a short filename that communicates the action (for e
actions.description: Provide a short filename that communicates the action (for example, `generate-readme.prompt.md` rather than `prompt1.prompt.md`).

actions.name: Start with an `#` level heading that matches the prompt inte
actions.description: Start with an `#` level heading that matches the prompt intent so it surfaces well in Quick Pick search.

actions.name: Write in direct, imperative sentences targeted at Copilot (f
actions.description: Write in direct, imperative sentences targeted at Copilot (for example, “Analyze”, “Generate”, “Summarize”).

actions.name: Specify the format, structure, and location of expected resu
actions.description: Specify the format, structure, and location of expected results (for example, “Create `docs/adr/adr-XXXX.md` using the template below”).

actions.name: Maintain reference tables (capabilities, status codes, role
actions.description: Maintain reference tables (capabilities, status codes, role descriptions) inline to keep the prompt self-contained. Update these tables when upstream

actions.name: [ ] Workflow covers preparation, execution, and post-process
actions.description: [ ] Workflow covers preparation, execution, and post-processing without gaps.

actions.name: [ ] Output expectations include formatting and storage detai
actions.description: [ ] Output expectations include formatting and storage details.

actions.name: [ ] Prompt executes successfully in VS Code (`Chat: Run Prom
actions.description: [ ] Prompt executes successfully in VS Code (`Chat: Run Prompt`) using representative scenarios.

actions.name: Version-control prompts alongside the code they affect; upda
actions.description: Version-control prompts alongside the code they affect; update them when dependencies, tooling, or review processes change.

actions.name: Coordinate with other repositories: when a prompt proves bro
actions.description: Coordinate with other repositories: when a prompt proves broadly useful, extract common guidance into instruction files or shared prompt packs.

rules.description: Every prompt file should include YAML frontmatter with the following fields:
Limit `tools` to the smallest set that enables the task. List them in the preferred execution order when the sequence matters.
Avoid idioms, humor, or culturally specific references; favor neutral, inclusive language.
Provide validation steps—manual checks, automated commands, or acceptance criteria lists—that reviewers can execute after running the prompt.
Embed Good/Bad examples or scaffolds (Markdown templates, JSON stubs) that the prompt should produce or follow.
[ ] Validation steps are actionable (commands, diff checks, review prompts).
Review prompts periodically to ensure tool lists, model requirements, and linked documents remain valid.

directives.description: Instructions for creating effective and maintainable prompt files that guide GitHub Copilot in delivering consistent, high-quality outcomes across any
Target audience: maintainers and contributors authoring reusable prompts for Copilot Chat.
Goals: predictable behaviour, clear expectations, minimal permissions, and portability across repositories.
Primary references: VS Code documentation on prompt files and organization-specific conventions.
| `description` | Recommended | A short description of the prompt (single sentence, actionable outcome) |
| `name` | Optional | The name shown after typing `/` in chat. Defaults to filename if not specified |
| `agent` | Recommended | The agent to use: `ask`, `edit`, `agent`, or a custom agent name. Defaults to current agent |
| `model` | Optional | The language model to use. Defaults to the currently selected model |
| `tools` | Optional | List of tool/tool set names available for this prompt |
| `argument-hint` | Optional | Hint text shown in chat input to guide user interaction |
